meta.description: Classification of Financial Instruments (CFI) code decoder

E.name: Equities
E.long: Equity instruments representing ownership interest in a company or entity

S.name: Shares (Common/Ordinary)
S.long: Common or ordinary shares representing basic ownership units in a corporation with standard shareholder rights

attribute1.label: Voting Right
attribute1.long: Defines the voting rights attached to this common/ordinary share at shareholder meetings

V.name: Voting
V.long: Share carries standard voting rights at shareholder meetings

N.name: Non-Voting
N.long: Share does not carry any voting rights

R.name: Restricted
R.long: Share carries restricted or limited voting rights

E.name: Enhanced Voting
E.long: Share carries enhanced or multiple voting rights per share

attribute2.label: Ownership
attribute2.long: Defines whether there are restrictions on who may own this common/ordinary share

T.name: Restrictions
T.long: There are restrictions on who may hold or transfer this share

U.name: Free
U.long: Share is freely transferable with no ownership restrictions

attribute3.label: Payment Status
attribute3.short: How much of the share price has been paid
attribute3.long: Indicates the payment status of this common/ordinary share — whether fully paid, partly paid, or nil paid

F.name: Fully Paid
F.long: The full issue price of the share has been paid

O.name: Nil Paid
O.long: No part of the issue price has been paid yet

P.name: Partly Paid
P.long: Only part of the issue price has been paid; further calls may be made

attribute4.label: Form
attribute4.long: The physical or legal form in which this common/ordinary share is issued and held

B.name: Bearer
B.long: Ownership is determined by possession of the physical certificate

R.name: Registered
R.long: Ownership is recorded in a register maintained by the issuer or registrar

N.name: Bearer/Registered
N.long: The share may exist in either bearer or registered form

M.name: Others (Misc.)
M.long: Other or miscellaneous form not covered by the standard categories

P.name: Preferred Shares
P.long: Preferred or preference shares that typically carry priority over common shares for dividends and/or liquidation proceeds

attribute1.label: Voting Right
attribute1.short: Voting rights of the preferred share
attribute1.long: Defines the voting rights attached to this preferred share at shareholder meetings

V.name: Voting
V.long: Preferred share carries voting rights at shareholder meetings

N.name: Non-Voting
N.long: Preferred share does not carry any voting rights

R.name: Restricted
R.long: Preferred share carries restricted or limited voting rights

E.name: Enhanced Voting
E.long: Preferred share carries enhanced or multiple voting rights

attribute2.label: Redemption
attribute2.long: Defines whether and how this preferred share can be redeemed, extended, or exchanged

R.name: Redeemable
R.long: The issuer or holder can redeem the preferred share at specified terms

E.name: Extendible
E.long: The maturity or redemption date can be extended

T.name: Redeemable/Extendible
T.long: The preferred share can be both redeemed and extended

G.name: Exchangeable
G.long: The preferred share can be exchanged for another instrument

A.name: Redeemable/Exchangeable/Extendible
A.long: The preferred share can be redeemed, exchanged, or extended

C.name: Redeemable/Exchangeable
C.long: The preferred share can be redeemed or exchanged

N.name: Perpetual
N.long: The preferred share has no maturity date and exists in perpetuity

attribute3.label: Income
attribute3.long: Defines the type of income or dividend payment structure of this preferred share

C.name: Cumulative, Fixed Rate
C.long: Pays a fixed rate dividend; unpaid dividends accumulate and must be paid before common dividends

P.name: Participating
P.long: Holder participates in additional profits beyond the stated dividend

Q.name: Cumulative, Participating
Q.long: Dividends accumulate if unpaid, and the holder also participates in additional profits

A.name: Adjustable/Variable Rate
A.long: Dividend rate adjusts based on a reference rate or formula

N.name: Normal Rate
N.long: Standard dividend rate as determined by the issuer

U.name: Auction Rate
U.long: Dividend rate is set through a periodic auction process

attribute4.label: Form
attribute4.long: The physical or legal form in which this preferred share is issued and held

B.name: Bearer
B.long: Ownership is determined by possession of the physical certificate

R.name: Registered
R.long: Ownership is recorded in a register

N.name: Bearer/Registered
N.long: May exist in either bearer or registered form

C.name: Convertible Shares
C.long: Common or ordinary shares that are convertible into another type of security

attribute1.label: Voting Right
attribute1.long: Defines the voting rights attached to this convertible share

E.name: Enhanced Voting
E.long: Carries enhanced or multiple voting rights

attribute2.label: Ownership
attribute2.long: Defines whether there are restrictions on who may own this convertible share

T.name: Restrictions
T.long: Restrictions apply on holding or transferring

attribute3.label: Payment Status
attribute3.long: Payment status of this convertible share

attribute4.label: Form
attribute4.long: The form in which this convertible share is issued

F.name: Preferred Convertible Shares
F.long: Preferred shares that are convertible into another type of security, combining preference rights with conversion features

attribute1.label: Voting Right
attribute1.long: Defines the voting rights attached to this preferred convertible share

attribute2.label: Redemption
attribute2.long: Defines redemption, extension, or exchange features of this preferred convertible share

R.name: Redeemable
R.long: Can be redeemed at specified terms

T.name: Redeemable/Extendible
T.long: Can be both redeemed and extended

G.name: Exchangeable
G.long: Can be exchanged for another instrument

A.name: Redeemable/Exchangeable/Extendible
A.long: Can be redeemed, exchanged, or extended

attribute3.label: Income
attribute3.long: Defines the income or dividend payment structure of this preferred convertible share

attribute4.label: Form
attribute4.long: The form in which this preferred convertible share is issued

L.name: Limited Partnership Units
L.long: Units representing limited partnership interests with limited liability

attribute1.label: Voting Right
attribute1.long: Defines the voting rights attached to this limited partnership unit

attribute2.label: Ownership
attribute2.long: Defines whether there are restrictions on who may own this LP unit

attribute3.label: Payment Status
attribute3.long: Payment status of this LP unit

attribute4.label: Form
attribute4.long: The form in which this LP unit is issued

D.name: Depository Receipts on Equities
D.long: Depository receipts representing ownership of equity instruments held by a depository bank

attribute1.label: Instrument Dependency
attribute1.long: Identifies the type of underlying equity instrument that this depository receipt represents

attribute2.label: Redemption/Conversion
attribute2.short: Redemption or conversion feature of underlying
attribute2.long: Defines whether the underlying asset of this depository receipt is redeemable, convertible, or perpetual

D.name: Convertible/Redeemable
D.long: Underlying asset is both convertible and redeemable

attribute3.label: Income
attribute3.long: Defines the income or dividend payment type for this equity depository receipt

attribute4.label: Form
attribute4.long: The form in which this depository receipt is issued

Y.name: Structured Instruments (Participation)
Y.long: Structured instruments offering participation in the performance of an underlying asset

attribute1.label: Type
attribute1.long: Defines the specific type of participation structured instrument

A.name: Tracker Certificate
A.long: Tracks the performance of the underlying asset 1:1

B.name: Outperforming Certificate
B.long: Provides leveraged participation in the upside of the underlying

C.name: Bonus Certificate
C.long: Offers a bonus payment if the underlying stays above a barrier

E.name: Twin-Win Certificate
E.long: Profits from both positive and negative movements of the underlying within a barrier

attribute2.label: Distribution
attribute2.long: Defines how distributions are paid on this participation structured instrument

attribute3.label: Repayment
attribute3.long: Defines how the principal is repaid at maturity for this structured instrument

V.name: Physical Repayment
V.long: Repaid by delivery of the underlying asset

E.name: Elect at Settlement
E.long: Holder or issuer elects cash or physical at settlement

attribute4.label: Underlying Asset
attribute4.long: Identifies the type of underlying asset for this participation structured instrument

B.name: Baskets
B.long: Based on a basket of assets

R.name: Preference Shares
R.long: Preference shares with priority rights, distinct from standard preferred shares in certain jurisdictions

attribute1.label: Voting Right
attribute1.long: Defines the voting rights attached to this preference share

attribute2.label: Redemption
attribute2.long: Defines redemption, extension, or exchange features of this preference share

R.name: Redeemable
R.long: Can be redeemed at specified terms

T.name: Redeemable/Extendible
T.long: Can be both redeemed and extended

G.name: Exchangeable
G.long: Can be exchanged for another instrument

A.name: Redeemable/Exchangeable/Extendible
A.long: Can be redeemed, exchanged, or extended

attribute3.label: Income
attribute3.long: Defines the income or dividend payment structure of this preference share

attribute4.label: Form
attribute4.long: The form in which this preference share is issued

V.name: Preference Convertible Shares
V.long: Preference shares that are convertible into another type of security

attribute1.label: Voting Right
attribute1.long: Defines the voting rights attached to this preference convertible share

attribute2.label: Redemption
attribute2.long: Defines redemption, extension, or exchange features of this preference convertible share

T.name: Redeemable/Extendible
T.long: Can be both redeemed and extended

A.name: Redeemable/Exchangeable/Extendible
A.long: Can be redeemed, exchanged, or extended

attribute3.label: Income
attribute3.long: Defines the income or dividend payment structure

attribute4.label: Form
attribute4.long: The form in which this preference convertible share is issued

U.name: Units (Unit Trusts, Mutual Funds, OPCVM/OICVM)
U.long: Units from unit trusts, mutual funds, or similar collective investment schemes (OPCVM/OICVM)

attribute1.label: Closed/Open-end
attribute1.long: Whether this fund unit is from a closed-end or open-end fund

C.name: Closed-end
C.long: Fixed number of units; traded on exchange

O.name: Open-end
O.long: Units can be created or redeemed on demand

attribute2.label: Distribution Policy
attribute2.long: Defines how the fund distributes income to unitholders

M.name: Mixed Funds
M.long: Combination of income distribution and accumulation

attribute3.label: Assets
attribute3.long: Primary asset class held by the fund

M.name: Mixed-General
M.long: Invests in a mix of asset types

attribute4.label: Form
attribute4.long: The form in which this fund unit is issued

M.name: Others (Misc.)
M.long: Other miscellaneous equity instruments not classified elsewhere

attribute1.label: Not Applicable
attribute1.long: Not applicable for miscellaneous equity instruments

attribute2.label: Not Applicable
attribute2.long: Not applicable for miscellaneous equity instruments

attribute3.label: Not Applicable
attribute3.long: Not applicable for miscellaneous equity instruments

attribute4.label: Form
attribute4.long: The form in which this instrument is issued

D.name: Debt Instruments
D.long: Debt instruments representing a borrowing arrangement where the issuer owes the holder a debt obligation

B.name: Bonds
B.long: Standard bond instruments representing a fixed-income debt security

attribute1.label: Type of Interest
attribute1.long: Defines the type of interest or coupon payment on this bond

F.name: Fixed Rate
F.long: Pays a fixed rate of interest

Z.name: Zero Rate/Discounted
Z.long: Issued at a discount with no periodic interest payments

V.name: Variable
V.long: Interest rate varies based on a reference rate

K.name: Payment in Kind
K.long: Interest paid in additional securities rather than cash

attribute2.label: Guarantee
attribute2.long: Defines the guarantee or seniority level of this bond

T.name: Government/State Guarantee
T.long: Guaranteed by a government or state entity

S.name: Secured
S.long: Backed by specific collateral or assets

U.name: Unsecured/Unguaranteed
U.long: Not backed by collateral or guarantee

P.name: Negative Pledge
P.long: Issuer agrees not to pledge assets to other creditors

N.name: Senior
N.long: Senior ranking in the capital structure

O.name: Senior Subordinated
O.long: Below senior but above junior debt

Q.name: Junior
Q.long: Junior ranking in the capital structure

attribute3.label: Redemption/Reimbursement
attribute3.long: Defines the maturity and redemption characteristics of this bond

G.name: Fixed Maturity with Call Feature
G.long: Fixed maturity; issuer can call before maturity

C.name: Fixed Maturity with Put Feature
C.long: Fixed maturity; holder can put before maturity

D.name: Fixed Maturity with Put and Call
D.long: Fixed maturity with both put and call features

L.name: Amortization Plan with Put and Call
L.long: Amortizing with both put and call features

attribute4.label: Form
attribute4.long: The form in which this bond is issued

C.name: Convertible Bonds
C.long: Bonds that can be converted into equity or another security at the holder's option

attribute1.label: Type of Interest
attribute1.long: Defines the type of interest or coupon payment on this convertible bond

F.name: Fixed Rate
F.long: Pays a fixed rate of interest

Z.name: Zero Rate/Discounted
Z.long: Issued at a discount with no periodic interest payments

V.name: Variable
V.long: Interest rate varies based on a reference rate

K.name: Payment in Kind
K.long: Interest paid in additional securities rather than cash

attribute2.label: Guarantee
attribute2.long: Defines the guarantee or seniority level of this convertible bond

T.name: Government/State Guarantee
T.long: Guaranteed by a government or state entity

S.name: Secured
S.long: Backed by specific collateral or assets

U.name: Unsecured/Unguaranteed
U.long: Not backed by collateral or guarantee

P.name: Negative Pledge
P.long: Issuer agrees not to pledge assets to other creditors

N.name: Senior
N.long: Senior ranking in the capital structure

O.name: Senior Subordinated
O.long: Below senior but above junior debt

Q.name: Junior
Q.long: Junior ranking in the capital structure

attribute3.label: Redemption/Reimbursement
attribute3.long: Defines the maturity and redemption characteristics of this convertible bond

G.name: Fixed Maturity with Call Feature
G.long: Fixed maturity; issuer can call before maturity

C.name: Fixed Maturity with Put Feature
C.long: Fixed maturity; holder can put before maturity

D.name: Fixed Maturity with Put and Call
D.long: Fixed maturity with both put and call features

L.name: Amortization Plan with Put and Call
L.long: Amortizing with both put and call features

attribute4.label: Form
attribute4.long: The form in which this convertible bond is issued

W.name: Bonds with Warrants Attached
W.long: Bonds issued with warrants attached, giving the holder the right to purchase additional securities

attribute1.label: Type of Interest
attribute1.long: Defines the type of interest or coupon payment on this bond with warrants

F.name: Fixed Rate
F.long: Pays a fixed rate of interest

Z.name: Zero Rate/Discounted
Z.long: Issued at a discount with no periodic interest payments

V.name: Variable
V.long: Interest rate varies based on a reference rate

K.name: Payment in Kind
K.long: Interest paid in additional securities rather than cash

attribute2.label: Guarantee
attribute2.long: Defines the guarantee or seniority level of this bond with warrants

T.name: Government/State Guarantee
T.long: Guaranteed by a government or state entity

S.name: Secured
S.long: Backed by specific collateral or assets

U.name: Unsecured/Unguaranteed
U.long: Not backed by collateral or guarantee

P.name: Negative Pledge
P.long: Issuer agrees not to pledge assets to other creditors

N.name: Senior
N.long: Senior ranking in the capital structure

O.name: Senior Subordinated
O.long: Below senior but above junior debt

Q.name: Junior
Q.long: Junior ranking in the capital structure

attribute3.label: Redemption/Reimbursement
attribute3.long: Defines the maturity and redemption characteristics of this bond with warrants

G.name: Fixed Maturity with Call Feature
G.long: Fixed maturity; issuer can call before maturity

C.name: Fixed Maturity with Put Feature
C.long: Fixed maturity; holder can put before maturity

D.name: Fixed Maturity with Put and Call
D.long: Fixed maturity with both put and call features

L.name: Amortization Plan with Put and Call
L.long: Amortizing with both put and call features

attribute4.label: Form
attribute4.long: The form in which this bond with warrants is issued

T.name: Medium-Term Notes
T.long: Medium-term notes — debt instruments typically issued on a continuous basis with varying maturities

attribute1.label: Type of Interest
attribute1.long: Defines the type of interest or coupon payment on this medium-term note

F.name: Fixed Rate
F.long: Pays a fixed rate of interest

Z.name: Zero Rate/Discounted
Z.long: Issued at a discount with no periodic interest payments

V.name: Variable
V.long: Interest rate varies based on a reference rate

K.name: Payment in Kind
K.long: Interest paid in additional securities rather than cash

attribute2.label: Guarantee
attribute2.long: Defines the guarantee or seniority level of this medium-term note

T.name: Government/State Guarantee
T.long: Guaranteed by a government or state entity

S.name: Secured
S.long: Backed by specific collateral or assets

U.name: Unsecured/Unguaranteed
U.long: Not backed by collateral or guarantee

P.name: Negative Pledge
P.long: Issuer agrees not to pledge assets to other creditors

N.name: Senior
N.long: Senior ranking in the capital structure

O.name: Senior Subordinated
O.long: Below senior but above junior debt

Q.name: Junior
Q.long: Junior ranking in the capital structure

attribute3.label: Redemption/Reimbursement
attribute3.long: Defines the maturity and redemption characteristics of this medium-term note

G.name: Fixed Maturity with Call Feature
G.long: Fixed maturity; issuer can call before maturity

C.name: Fixed Maturity with Put Feature
C.long: Fixed maturity; holder can put before maturity

D.name: Fixed Maturity with Put and Call
D.long: Fixed maturity with both put and call features

L.name: Amortization Plan with Put and Call
L.long: Amortizing with both put and call features

attribute4.label: Form
attribute4.long: The form in which this medium-term note is issued

S.name: Structured Products (Capital Protection)
S.long: Structured products that include capital protection, guaranteeing return of at least part of the invested capital

attribute1.label: Type
attribute1.long: Defines the specific type of capital-protected structured product

A.name: Capital Protection Certificate with Participation
A.long: Capital protected with participation in underlying performance

C.name: Barrier Capital Protection Certificate
C.long: Capital protection subject to barrier conditions

attribute2.label: Distribution
attribute2.long: Defines how distributions are paid on this capital-protected structured product

attribute3.label: Repayment
attribute3.long: Defines the repayment method at maturity for this capital-protected structured product

F.name: Fixed Cash Repayment (Only Protected Capital Level)
F.long: Repaid in cash at the protected capital level only

V.name: Variable Cash Repayment
V.long: Cash repayment amount varies based on underlying performance

attribute4.label: Underlying Asset
attribute4.long: Identifies the underlying asset class for this capital-protected structured product

B.name: Baskets
B.long: Based on a basket of assets

D.name: Debt Instruments/Interest Rates
D.long: Based on debt instruments or interest rates

E.name: Structured Products (Without Capital Protection)
E.long: Structured products without capital protection — the investor's capital is fully at risk

attribute1.label: Type
attribute1.long: Defines the specific type of structured product without capital protection

A.name: Discount Certificate
A.long: Purchased at a discount to the underlying with capped upside

B.name: Barrier Discount Certificate
B.long: Discount certificate with a barrier feature

C.name: Reverse Convertible
C.long: Pays a coupon but may repay in shares if underlying falls below strike

D.name: Barrier Reverse Convertible
D.long: Reverse convertible with a barrier condition

E.name: Express Certificate
E.long: May be called early if underlying reaches certain levels

attribute2.label: Repayment
attribute2.long: Defines the repayment method at maturity for this non-protected structured product

C.name: Repayment in Assets and Cash
C.long: Repaid in a combination of assets and cash

T.name: Repayment in Assets or Cash
T.long: Repaid in either assets or cash

attribute3.label: Not Applicable
attribute3.long: Not applicable for this instrument type

attribute4.label: Not Applicable
attribute4.long: Not applicable for this instrument type

G.name: Mortgage-Backed Securities (MBS)
G.long: Mortgage-backed securities — debt instruments backed by a pool of mortgage loans

attribute1.label: Type of Interest
attribute1.long: Defines the type of interest payment on this MBS

F.name: Fixed Rate
F.long: Pays a fixed rate of interest

Z.name: Zero Rate/Discounted
Z.long: Issued at a discount with no periodic interest

attribute2.label: Guarantee
attribute2.long: Defines the guarantee or seniority level of this MBS

attribute3.label: Redemption/Reimbursement
attribute3.long: Defines the maturity and redemption characteristics of this MBS

D.name: Fixed Maturity with Put and Call

L.name: Amortization Plan with Put and Call

attribute4.label: Form
attribute4.long: The form in which this MBS is issued

A.name: Asset-Backed Securities (ABS)
A.long: Asset-backed securities — debt instruments backed by a pool of non-mortgage assets

attribute1.label: Type of Interest
attribute1.long: Defines the type of interest payment on this ABS

attribute2.label: Guarantee
attribute2.long: Defines the guarantee or seniority level of this ABS

attribute3.label: Redemption/Reimbursement
attribute3.long: Defines the maturity and redemption characteristics of this ABS

D.name: Fixed Maturity with Put and Call

L.name: Amortization Plan with Put and Call

attribute4.label: Form
attribute4.long: The form in which this ABS is issued

N.name: Municipal Bonds
N.long: Municipal bonds — debt instruments issued by local governments or their agencies

attribute1.label: Type of Interest
attribute1.long: Defines the type of interest payment on this municipal bond

attribute2.label: Guarantee
attribute2.long: Defines the guarantee or seniority level of this municipal bond

attribute3.label: Redemption/Reimbursement
attribute3.long: Defines the maturity and redemption characteristics of this municipal bond

D.name: Fixed Maturity with Put and Call

L.name: Amortization Plan with Put and Call

attribute4.label: Form
attribute4.long: The form in which this municipal bond is issued

D.name: Depository Receipts on Debt Instruments
D.long: Depository receipts representing ownership of debt instruments held by a depository bank

attribute1.label: Instrument Dependency
attribute1.long: Identifies the type of underlying debt instrument that this depository receipt represents

attribute2.label: Type of Interest/Cash Payment
attribute2.long: Defines the interest or coupon type of the underlying debt instrument

attribute3.label: Guarantee
attribute3.long: Defines the guarantee or seniority level of the underlying debt

attribute4.label: Redemption/Reimbursement
attribute4.long: Defines the maturity and redemption characteristics of the underlying debt

D.name: Fixed Maturity with Put and Call

L.name: Amortization Plan with Put and Call

Y.name: Money Market Instruments
Y.long: Short-term debt instruments traded in money markets

attribute1.label: Type of Interest
attribute1.long: Defines the interest type on this money market instrument

attribute2.label: Guarantee
attribute2.long: Defines the guarantee or seniority level of this money market instrument

attribute3.label: Not Applicable
attribute3.long: Not applicable for money market instruments

attribute4.label: Form
attribute4.long: The form in which this money market instrument is issued

M.name: Others (Misc.)
M.long: Other miscellaneous debt instruments not classified elsewhere

attribute1.label: Type
attribute1.long: Specific type of miscellaneous debt instrument

attribute2.label: Not Applicable
attribute2.long: Not applicable

attribute3.label: Not Applicable
attribute3.long: Not applicable

attribute4.label: Form
attribute4.long: The form in which this instrument is issued

C.name: Collective Investment Vehicles
C.long: Collective investment vehicles including funds, ETFs, REITs, and other pooled investment structures

I.name: Standard (Vanilla) Investment Funds/Mutual Funds
I.long: Standard investment funds or mutual funds that pool capital from multiple investors

attribute1.label: Closed/Open-end
attribute1.long: Whether this fund is closed-end, open-end, or other

O.name: Open-End
O.long: Units can be created or redeemed on demand at NAV

C.name: Closed-End
C.long: Fixed number of shares; traded on exchange

attribute2.label: Distribution Policy
attribute2.long: Defines how the fund distributes or reinvests income

attribute3.label: Asset
attribute3.long: Primary asset class that this fund invests in

L.name: Mixed
L.long: Invests in a mix of asset classes

attribute4.label: Security Type and Investor Restrictions
attribute4.long: Defines the type of security issued and any investor qualification restrictions

S.name: Shares
S.long: Issued as shares, open to all investors

Q.name: Shares for QI
Q.long: Issued as shares, restricted to qualified investors

U.name: Units
U.long: Issued as units, open to all investors

Y.name: Units for QI
Y.long: Issued as units, restricted to qualified investors

H.name: Hedge Funds
H.long: Hedge funds employing alternative investment strategies

attribute1.label: Investment Strategy
attribute1.long: The core hedge fund investment strategy characterizing the fund

D.name: Directional
D.long: Takes directional bets on market movements

R.name: Relative Value
R.long: Exploits pricing differences between related instruments

S.name: Security Selection
S.long: Selects individual securities based on analysis

E.name: Event-Driven
E.long: Invests based on corporate events (mergers, restructuring, etc.)

A.name: Arbitrage
A.long: Seeks to profit from price discrepancies

attribute2.label: Not Applicable
attribute2.long: Not applicable for hedge funds

attribute3.label: Not Applicable
attribute3.long: Not applicable for hedge funds

attribute4.label: Not Applicable
attribute4.long: Not applicable for hedge funds

B.name: Real Estate Investment Trusts (REITs)
B.long: Real estate investment trusts that invest in income-producing real estate

attribute1.label: Closed/Open-end
attribute1.long: Whether this REIT is closed-end or open-end

attribute2.label: Distribution Policy
attribute2.long: Defines how the REIT distributes income

attribute3.label: Not Applicable
attribute3.long: Not applicable for REITs

attribute4.label: Security Type and Investor Restrictions
attribute4.long: Defines the type of security issued and any investor restrictions for this REIT

E.name: Exchange-Traded Funds (ETFs)
E.long: Exchange-traded funds that trade on exchanges like stocks while tracking an index, commodity, or basket

attribute1.label: Closed/Open-end
attribute1.long: Whether this ETF is closed-end or open-end

attribute2.label: Distribution Policy
attribute2.long: Defines how the ETF distributes income

attribute3.label: Asset
attribute3.long: Primary asset class that this ETF tracks or invests in

attribute4.label: Security Type and Investor Restrictions
attribute4.long: Defines the type of security issued for this ETF

S.name: Pension Funds
S.long: Pension funds providing retirement benefits to members

attribute1.label: Closed/Open-end
attribute1.long: Whether this pension fund is closed-end or open-end

attribute2.label: Strategy/Style
attribute2.long: The investment strategy or style of this pension fund

L.name: Life Style
L.long: Strategy changes depending on age group of members

attribute3.label: Type
attribute3.long: Defines whether the pension is defined benefit or defined contribution

R.name: Defined Benefit
R.long: Benefits are predefined based on salary/service

B.name: Defined Contribution
B.long: Contributions are defined; benefits depend on investment returns

attribute4.label: Security Type and Investor Restrictions
attribute4.long: Defines the type of security issued for this pension fund

F.name: Funds of Funds
F.long: Funds that invest in other funds rather than directly in securities

attribute1.label: Closed/Open-end
attribute1.long: Whether this fund of funds is closed-end or open-end

attribute2.label: Distribution Policy
attribute2.long: Defines how this fund of funds distributes income

attribute3.label: Type of Funds
attribute3.long: Defines the type of underlying funds that this fund of funds invests in

attribute4.label: Security Type and Investor Restrictions
attribute4.long: Defines the security type and investor restrictions for this fund of funds

P.name: Private Equity Funds
P.long: Private equity funds investing in non-publicly traded companies

attribute1.label: Closed/Open-end
attribute1.long: Whether this PE fund is closed-end or open-end

attribute2.label: Distribution Policy
